justifications.clarity: Prose is well-paced and consistently uses motivating context boxes before formal definitions. Definitions precede their first use throughout, and transitions between sections are smooth. The previously flagged structural confusion in Section 3 — where all four subsections (Skills, Hooks, Frameworks, Multi-Agent) were enclosed in a single \begin{context} block — has been corrected: the motivating paragraph now occupies its own \begin{context} environment (lines 556–567) and the formal subsections are correctly enclosed in \begin{deepdive} (lines 569–833). This eliminates the clarity impediment noted in the previous review. No other structural issues affect readability.
justifications.rigor: All six numbered equations remain mathematically correct: cosine retrieval (Eq. 1), RAG marginalisation (Eq. 2), ANN approximation factor bound (Eq. 3), BM25 score (Eq. 4), hybrid search linear combination (Eq. 5), and citation-verified answer set (Eq. 6). The RRF formula is stated correctly. All core definitions — PRA loop, ReAct trajectory, Reflexion loop, tool, robust tool calling, skill, agent hook, multi-agent system, chunking strategy, RAG, ANN, BM25, RAGAS metrics, latency budget, audit trail — are internally consistent and grounded in cited literature. The minor empirical claims without citations identified in the previous review persist: the '60% retrieval-reduction' figure in Example 5, the '15–20% chunking improvement', the '94% recall' in Example 10, the 'alpha ≈ 0.7' heuristic, and the '10–60 second' signal-staleness figure remain uncited. These are small deductions in an otherwise rigorously grounded chapter.
justifications.completeness: All eight learning objectives are addressed: (1) PRA cycle vs. single-turn inference in Sec. 1.1; (2) ReAct loop with financial example in Sec. 1.2 and Example 3; (3) custom tool design in Sec. 2.2 and Listing 1; (4) three memory types in Sec. 1.3; (5) RAG pipeline and RAGAS evaluation in Sec. 4; (6) LangChain/LlamaIndex/AutoGen comparison with latency and cost discussion in Secs. 3.3 and 5.1; (7) five finance-specific applications (earnings analysis, report generation, portfolio assistance, filing search, trading signals), exceeding the required three; (8) prompt injection attacks and HITL governance patterns in Sec. 6. Exercise set provides [B] (ReAct price lookup), [I] (RAG retrieval ranking), and [A] (multi-agent pipeline design), satisfying all required difficulty tiers. No placeholder text is present anywhere in the file.
justifications.pedagogy: The chapter is pedagogically strong. Eight learning objectives are stated upfront in a clearly formatted remark box. Difficulty escalates systematically: informal motivation (context boxes) → formal definitions → worked examples → pseudocode/listings → multi-agent design exercises. Six worked examples (PRA for earnings monitoring, ReAct for filing retrieval, financial DB tool suite, embedding a wealth-management corpus, earnings pipeline output, portfolio Q&A) and a fully runnable two-agent code listing anchor abstract material in concrete finance tasks. The Reflexion and SPLADE extensions are introduced as natural elaborations after foundational concepts are established. The Remark on signal staleness and the Regulatory Perspective remark function as effective 'stop and reflect' interjections. Three exercises covering all required difficulty levels are present, with the advanced exercise requiring implementation and adversarial analysis. The Section 3 deepdive fix restores proper pedagogical layering for the Skills/Hooks/Frameworks/Multi-Agent content.
justifications.style: Academic register is consistent throughout; no \paragraph{} or \textbf{} inline paragraph labels appear anywhere. Citations use \citet{} and \cite{} correctly and uniformly. Notation is consistent across all definitions. Both style issues identified in the previous review have been corrected: (1) Section 3 now correctly uses a standalone \begin{context}...\end{context} for the motivating paragraph followed by a \begin{deepdive}...\end{deepdive} that encloses all four formal subsections (Skills, Hooks, Frameworks, Multi-Agent) and the two-agent listing, consistent with every other section in the chapter; (2) the two-agent pipeline listing now uses \begin{listing}[H]...\end{listing} with an inline \caption{} and \label{} consistent with Listings 1 and 3. A minor residual issue: Sections 5 (Finance-Specific Applications, lines 1172–1450) and 6 (Governance, lines 1457–1688) are each wrapped entirely in a single \begin{context} environment that contains formal Definitions (Latency Budget, Audit Trail) and structured lists that would conventionally belong in deepdive. This is a lesser concern — applications and governance prose are more narrative than the core theory sections — but it introduces a slight inconsistency with the chapter's own layering convention.
